human_verification.notes: Reviewed the manifest, source policy, evaluation suite, deployment package, and marketing notes against issue #155 and the repository delivery contract.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary stays inside UI-driven robotic process automation for legacy and hard-to-integrate application surfaces and explicitly rejects adjacent API integration, low-code workflow, scheduler ownership, and software rewrite work.

Manifest records real public authoritative sources from UiPath, Microsoft, Automation Anywhere, SS&C Blue Prism, and SAP with publisher, authority rationale, review date, refresh interval, and decay policy.

The local package defines RPA-specific evaluation scenarios, migration-risk guidance, low-confidence escalation rules, and shared-meta-agent fallback behavior that do not depend on delegated corpus retrieval.
delegated_meta_agent_evidence: Best-practice corpus expansion and source-policy normalization are delegated to shared meta-agents #225 and #306 when available, with safe fallback to the local reviewed baseline.
Evaluation normalization, readiness consistency review, deployment normalization, and commercialization normalization are delegated to #302 through #305 without replacing specialist-owned evidence.
Boundary review and scaffold consistency checks are delegated to #307 and #308 only as optional post-startup augmentation.
notes: Structured evidence supports the validated claim required by issue #155.
Evaluation thresholds exceed the minimum issue requirements with 12 scenarios and a 1.00 pass rate against a 0.92 threshold.
Deployment notes document runtime model, tenant isolation, monitoring and logging expectations, rollback path, and deferred shared-meta-agent initialization.
Marketing readiness notes document buyer profile, packaging, subscription model, proof points, and go-to-market gaps without widening the technical boundary.
